summary: Drew DeVault announces he has broken even on doing free software full-time, thanks to donations and paid SourceHut accounts. He provides a wide-ranging status update covering aerc email client progress, Wayland VR support via DRM leasing, SourceHut developments including Alpine Linux's migration to lists.sr.ht, and his ongoing Wayland book. The post serves as both a milestone celebration and a detailed accounting of what supporter funding has produced across multiple projects.
key_points: DeVault has reached financial sustainability doing free software full-time, a personal milestone funded by donations and SourceHut subscriptions
The aerc email client is rapidly gaining features including maildir support, tab completion, OAuth, and search — approaching a 0.2.0 release
DRM leasing for Wayland VR is being implemented across the full stack: Wayland protocol, Vulkan extension, wlroots, sway, mesa, and AMD's radv driver
Alpine Linux has migrated its mailing list infrastructure to a self-hosted lists.sr.ht instance, validating SourceHut as production-ready infrastructure
His 'make a blog' initiative created 13 new blogs and spawned the free writers club mailing list to encourage tech writing
money_quotes: I have officially broken even on doing free software full time!
I've dreamed of sustainably working on free software for a long, long time, and I'm very grateful for all of your support in helping realize that dream.
Now let me share with you what your money has bought over the past month!
The result: a working VR demo on Sway.
Our work continues to evolve into the most robust Wayland implementation available today, and I can only see it getting stronger.
The world is now 13 blogs richer for it.
key_insight: Sustainable full-time free software work is achievable through community funding, and the breadth of output — from email clients to VR protocols to community infrastructure — demonstrates what that model can produce.
